title: Small Rustic X Console
summary: A compact console table with a decorative X brace built into the back — one angled cut defines the whole look, everything else is a straightforward 2x4 frame.
description:
This is a small footprint version of the rustic X-console style: two 2x4 leg frames joined by stretchers, a 1x12 shelf, and a three-board 2x6 top. The X brace on the back is applied after the frame is built, not structural — it's what turns a plain leg frame into something with visual interest from across the room.

The X is two pieces, not one: a long diagonal cut with both ends parallel, and two shorter pieces angled from the same centerline to complete the pattern. Cut the long piece first and dry-fit it before cutting the two shorter pieces, since their length depends on exactly where the long piece lands. Decorative bolts through the leg-to-leg joints are a nice touch if you want exposed hardware as part of the look — the pocket holes underneath work just as well hidden.

tools.note: For the 45° angled cuts on the X brace pieces.

cutList.note: Cut to match the actual width of the 1x12 shelf boards.
Both ends cut at 45°, ends ARE parallel — long point to short point measurement.
One end cut at 45°, long-point measurement.

steps.title: Build the front and back frames
steps.body: Build two frames — front and back — from the legs and front/back trim. The lower trim piece attaches 5" up from the bottom of the leg.

steps.title: Join the two frames with end trim
steps.body: Measure your actual 1x12 shelf board — it should be 11-1/4" wide, but cut the end trim to match whatever it actually measures. Attach the two frames together with the end trim pieces, hiding the pocket holes on the top and bottom edges. Decorative bolts can substitute for pocket screws here if you want exposed hardware.

steps.title: Install the shelf
steps.body: Set your pocket hole jig for 3/4" material (finish any 1-1/2" setting holes first, since resetting the jig partway through is easy to forget to reset back). Drill two pocket holes on the ends of the shelf and three along its long edges. Attach the shelf flush to the top of the lower trim with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Fit the X brace
steps.body: Dry-fit the long diagonal piece in the back of the console before gluing — its exact position determines the length of the two shorter pieces. Attach the long piece first with glue, then fit and glue the two shorter pieces to complete the X.

steps.title: Build and attach the top
steps.body:
Join the three top boards edge to edge with 1-1/2" pocket holes and 2-1/2" pocket hole screws. Attach the assembled top to the console from underneath with 2-1/2" screws through the 2x2s and 2x4s.

Sand everything and finish.

images.alt: Small rustic console table with an X brace back
